chapter_title.ar: باب صَلَاةِ الْعِيدَيْنِ وَ الْخُطْبَةِ فِيهِمَا
chapter_title.en: Chapter 88 – Salāt of the two Eids and the sermon in these two

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرفوع - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/436)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرفوع - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/436)
text: السُّنَّةُ عَلَى أَهْلِ الْأَمْصَارِ أَنْ يَبْرُزُوا مِنْ أَمْصَارِهِمْ فِي الْعِيدَيْنِ إِلَّا أَهْلَ مَكَّةَ فَإِنَّهُمْ يُصَلُّونَ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ الْحَرَامِ .

en.hubeali: (It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘The Sunnah upon the people of the city is that they should be going out from their cities during the two Eids, except for the people of Makkah, for they would be praying Salāt in the Sacred Masjid’.
en.sarwar: 10. Muhammad ibn Yahya in a marfu‘ manner has narrated from the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘It is of Sunnah for the people of the cities to go out in the open on both ‘Id days except people of Makkah; they perform Salah (prayer) in Masjid al-Haram.’”
